entry.title: Writing A (covert) Dynamic Loader in x86-64 MASM [0x02]

link.title: Writing A (covert) Dynamic Loader in x86-64 MASM [0x02]

entry.summary: [0x02] - The Problem Imports: As the analysts we’d like to quickly get an idea of what this binary is doing statically. So we sit down, open our favorite tool and check what kind of file it is, what libraries it depends on and what functions it calls: Imports tab in IDA Looks like this executable will perform dynamic linking at runtime, which is generally a red flag when dealing w...

entry.title: Writing A (covert) Dynamic Loader in x86-64 MASM [0x01]

link.title: Writing A (covert) Dynamic Loader in x86-64 MASM [0x01]

entry.summary: [0x01] - General Code Structure What we’re doing: How we’re doing it: We’ll start by declaring the only two imports we require to bootstrap our loader. ; HMODULE GetModuleHandleA(LPCSTR modulename) EXTRN __imp_GetModuleHandleA:PROC ; FARPROC GetProcAddress (HMODULE modulehandle, LPCSTR procname) EXTRN __imp_GetProcAddress:PROC The next step is to prepare some stati...

entry.title: Writing A (covert) Dynamic Loader in x86-64 MASM [0x00]

link.title: Writing A (covert) Dynamic Loader in x86-64 MASM [0x00]

entry.summary: [0x00] - Introduction Assumptions about the reader’s knowledge: Understanding of C code (pointers, structs, control flow …); Some Python3 experience (basic operations); Some Assembly experience (g.p. registers, stack layout, control flow …); Basic computer usage knowledge. Goals of this series: Get a working POC that when inspected statically gives nothing away; Avoi...
